messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "intelligent-subset-inflation",
 "title": "The €300M base counts the non-intelligent majority of a market the venture cannot sell into",
 "narrative": "The venture sells intelligent rack PDUs, but tamBase anchors on Grand View's $2.81B 'rack PDU' figure — and Grand View itself states the NON-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025. IndexBox confirms the split is real by selling 'Rack PDUs' and 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' as separate EU reports. The scope-down chain carefully audited geography and FX but never audited product type: roughly half the €300M base is basic/metered units the product line does not address. TAM, SAM and YAM all inherit a ~2x product-scope inflation while the ledger label ('rack-PDU market, all segments/buyers') reads as if it were scope-matched.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Grand View's own segmentation of the $2.81B anchor reports the non-intelligent rack-PDU segment held the largest 2025 revenue share, while the venture's product is intelligent PDUs only",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c2",
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "profile:Product"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Independent sizings of the intelligent-only subset run $2.1B (GIA 2024) to $3.0B (MRF 2025) against $1.7–3.9B for total rack-PDU — and IndexBox maintains distinct EU reports for 'Rack PDUs' vs 'Intelligent Rack PDUs', confirming the vendor community treats them as different products with different bases",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c12",
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c13",
    "digest:tam-base#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The €300M CE base carries the full-category value; the venture-relevant intelligent-only base is materially smaller, and every downstream funnel stage (SAM €165M, YAM €1.65M) inherits the inflation because serviceableFactor haircuts reach, not product scope",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The label 'rack-PDU market' looks perfectly scope-matched to a rack-PDU vendor — the refinement cycle even proved its diligence by removing the PDUs+PSUs ceiling, creating the impression definitional contamination was handled. The remaining contamination sits inside the anchor's own segmentation table, one level below the headline number anyone reads.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A scope-pure European source (IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs headline, or an Omdia segment table) showing intelligent/monitored/switched PDUs at ≥75% of European rack-PDU revenue — i.e., Europe's revenue mix is intelligent-dominated even if global unit mix is not.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "The anchor source directly states non-intelligent held the largest revenue share globally; the offset is that intelligent units carry 3–5x ASPs and European colo/hyperscale buyers skew intelligent, so the revenue-weighted European intelligent share plausibly lands at 45–60% rather than below 40%",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.55,
   "note": "Restrict the base to the intelligent-PDU-relevant revenue share of the rack-PDU category (~55% revenue-weighted in Europe)"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Intelligent vs non-intelligent revenue split in the next Grand View / Mordor rack-PDU report vintage",
   "where": "Grand View Research rack-PDU report segment tables; Mordor Intelligence Intelligent PDU report",
   "threshold": "Non-intelligent still ≥45% of European revenue",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "ASP spread between basic/metered and switched/outlet-metered SKUs on incumbent price lists",
   "where": "Vertiv Geist, Raritan/Legrand, and APC published EU price lists",
   "threshold": "Basic-unit volumes dominating distributor stock at <€500 ASP while intelligent SKUs sit >€1,500",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs report and difference it against the general EU Rack PDUs report to isolate the venture-relevant base",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "Two $4,000 reports collapse the product-scope ambiguity that the ±40% band silently absorbs"
  },
  {
   "action": "Add a metered-only downmarket SKU to the roadmap so the non-intelligent half of the base is addressable rather than excluded",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Converts a TAM-definition problem into a product-line decision"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "definition-scopedown",
 "riskId": "risk.definition-scopedown.intelligent-subset-inflation"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

IndexBox confirms the split is real by selling 'Rack PDUs' and 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' as separate EU reports. The scope-down chain carefully audited geography and FX but never audited product type: roughly half the €300M base is basic/metered units the product line does not address. Mordor Intelligence Intelligent PDU report","threshold":"Non-intelligent still ≥45% of European revenue","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"ASP spread between basic/metered and switched/outlet-metered SKUs on incumbent price lists","where":"Vertiv Geist, Raritan/Legrand, and APC published EU price lists","threshold":"Basic-unit volumes dominating distributor stock at <€500 ASP while intelligent SKUs sit >€1,500","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs report and difference it against the general EU Rack PDUs report to isolate the venture-relevant base","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Two $4,000 reports collapse the product-scope ambiguity that the ±40% band silently absorbs"},{"action":"Add a metered-only downmarket SKU to the roadmap so the non-intelligent half of the base is addressable rather than excluded","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Converts a TAM-definition problem into a product-line decision"}]}
